text:
# Carcavelos Daily Brief – Saturday, May 2nd

**Tiny but clean – classic spring conditions for a mellow session.**

**Morning (sunrise to 11am):** Expect 1.2–1.3m waves with glassy conditions. Head out between 05:00–08:00 for the cleanest glass before wind picks up. Light winds shift from west to south, keeping offshore/sideshore through breakfast time.

**Through the day:** Wind gradually builds from SSW (13–14km/h by mid-morning), turning slightly onshore by early afternoon. Wave height holds steady around 1.3m but becomes a bit choppy after 10am. Swell period softens from 9.7s down to 7.8s—still rideable, just less organised.

**Afternoon (12pm–sunset):** Conditions remain playable but windier and textured. Temperature climbs to 18°C—pleasant air.

**Tips:** Go early for glass. Soft-top or fish works best for these small peelers. Post-work session (4–5pm) still fun if you don't mind texture. 🤙
